outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump is supposed to get Congress' approval when the Iran war hits 60 days. Lawmakers can't agree when that is
outlets.summary: CNN frames the War Powers confusion as constitutional crisis. Lawmakers disagree whether 60-day clock started Feb 28 or March 2. Trump admin claims ceasefire 'terminates' hostilities. Legal experts dispute.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
outlets.summary: NPR focuses on the disconnect between the official $25B price tag and projected long-term costs of $630B-$1T. Emphasizes bipartisan frustration with lack of exit strategy.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Hegseth testifies on Iran war's $25B price tag as 60-day War Powers deadline looms
outlets.summary: Fox frames Hegseth as defending the war effort against partisan attacks. Highlights Sen. Kelly pressing on munitions depletion. Hegseth backtracks on earlier $200B supplemental funding request.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Republican senators eye vote on authorizing further military force in Iran
outlets.summary: Conservative outlet covers Murkowski's AUMF plan as responsible Republican governance rather than anti-war dissent. Frames it as constitutional duty, not opposition to Trump.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Could the US-Iran war become a protracted 'frozen' conflict?
outlets.summary: Analysis piece examining whether ceasefire + blockade creates indefinite frozen conflict. Notes parallels with Korean War armistice. Iran rebuilding capacity while US maintains siege.

outlets.name: BBC/Reuters
outlets.headline: Trump approval dips to record low 34% amid Iran war, inflation woes
outlets.summary: Reuters/Ipsos poll: Trump at 34% approval (record low). Only 22% approve of cost-of-living handling. Gas prices, war fatigue, and economic anxiety driving decline.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: In defiant message, Mojtaba Khamenei signals new chapter for Iran's control of Hormuz
outlets.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei's statement declaring 'future without America' in the Gulf analyzed as succession consolidation move. Only place for US 'at the bottom of its waters.' ToI frames it as direct challenge to Maritime Freedom Construct and CENTCOM options.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: 750,000 Israelis Unemployed Amid Wars With Iran, Hezbollah in Lebanon
outlets.summary: Broad unemployment 16.2%; 750,000 Israelis without work as wartime economy strains. Defense ~8% of GDP. Debt-to-GDP projected 70.5% by year end.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: 'A new chapter': Leader says Iran will determine 'new legal rules' in Strait of Hormuz
outlets.summary: Persian Gulf Day message from Mojtaba Khamenei: 'future without America' in Hormuz, vows to protect nuclear and missile capabilities as 'national assets.' Iran framing US blockade as already defeated.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Iran has every right to counter US's state-sponsored piracy, terrorism: UN envoy
outlets.summary: Iran's UN envoy frames US naval blockade as 'state-sponsored piracy.' Argues international law gives Iran full right to retaliate. Doubles down on Pezeshkian's 'doomed to fail' message.
